name: OAuth2/OIDC + short-lived JWT
description: Use OIDC identity with short access tokens and refresh rotation
when_to_use: User-facing apps SaaS platforms and third-party identity
trade_offs: JWT revocation needs token versioning or server-side metadata
implementation_notes: Access token 5-15m refresh rotation secure httpOnly cookies where browser-based
keywords: oauth oidc jwt refresh token auth

name: RBAC with scoped permissions
description: Role and permission checks enforced in service layer
when_to_use: Admin panels multi-tenant SaaS and team-based products
trade_offs: Can become too coarse for complex policy
implementation_notes: Check tenant scope and action permission before mutation

name: ABAC policy checks
description: Attribute-based authorization for context-sensitive access
when_to_use: Regulated apps document access and complex enterprise permissions
trade_offs: More complex to test and explain
implementation_notes: Centralize policy evaluation and add decision logs

name: Argon2id password hashing
description: Memory-hard password hashing for credential storage
when_to_use: Any system storing passwords
trade_offs: Requires parameter tuning and migration path
implementation_notes: Use unique salts pepper in secret manager and rehash on login when params change

name: Layered rate limiting
description: Limit abusive traffic by user tenant IP route and token
when_to_use: Login signup public APIs webhooks and expensive endpoints
trade_offs: Can block legitimate traffic if keys are too broad
implementation_notes: Apply stricter limits to auth and write endpoints with clear 429 response
keywords: rate limit brute force abuse api

name: Schema validation at boundary
description: Reject malformed input before business logic
when_to_use: All HTTP queue webhook and job inputs
trade_offs: Validation drift if schemas are duplicated
implementation_notes: Use DTO/schema validators and preserve domain invariants in services
keywords: input validation schema zod pydantic dto

name: Secret manager integration
description: Load secrets from managed secret store or environment injection
when_to_use: Production apps CI/CD containers and cloud deployments
trade_offs: Requires rotation and local development policy
implementation_notes: Never hardcode secrets or commit real values; rotate on exposure
keywords: secret manager env api key jwt secret

name: HMAC webhook verification
description: Verify third-party webhook signatures and timestamps
when_to_use: Payments billing email delivery and integration callbacks
trade_offs: Requires provider-specific canonical payload handling
implementation_notes: Validate raw body timestamp replay window and idempotency key

name: httpOnly secure cookies
description: Store browser session tokens in secure httpOnly cookies
when_to_use: Browser apps using cookie-based auth
trade_offs: Requires CSRF strategy and SameSite tuning
implementation_notes: Use Secure HttpOnly SameSite and CSRF protection for unsafe methods

name: CSRF token protection
description: Prevent cross-site form or cookie-authenticated write attacks
when_to_use: Cookie-authenticated browser apps
trade_offs: Token handling adds client and server coordination
implementation_notes: Use SameSite plus synchronizer or double-submit tokens

name: Strict CORS allowlist
description: Restrict browser origins that can read API responses
when_to_use: Public APIs consumed by browser clients
trade_offs: Incorrect wildcard with credentials leaks data
implementation_notes: Allow explicit origins methods headers and avoid credentials with wildcard

name: Field-level encryption
description: Encrypt especially sensitive fields separately from disk encryption
when_to_use: PII PHI tokens financial identifiers and regulated data
trade_offs: Search and filtering become harder
implementation_notes: Encrypt at application boundary and manage keys in KMS

name: Security audit events
description: Record authentication authorization and sensitive changes
when_to_use: Admin actions compliance and incident response
trade_offs: Logging too much can leak sensitive data
implementation_notes: Log actor action target result and reason without secrets

name: Tenant boundary enforcement
description: Guarantee every data access is scoped to tenant context
when_to_use: Multi-tenant SaaS and enterprise workspaces
trade_offs: Requires pervasive tests and central helpers
implementation_notes: Apply tenant filters in repositories plus database RLS where possible

name: Software composition analysis
description: Detect vulnerable dependencies before deploy
when_to_use: Any backend using package managers and containers
trade_offs: Can produce noisy findings
implementation_notes: Run SCA in CI and prioritize reachable critical vulnerabilities
keywords: dependency scan sca vulnerability snyk npm pip

name: Security headers baseline
description: Set browser-facing defensive headers for API and app responses
when_to_use: Web apps and APIs served to browsers
trade_offs: Headers vary by frontend embedding needs
implementation_notes: Use HSTS CSP frame-ancestors nosniff and permissions-policy

name: Refresh token rotation
description: Rotate refresh token on every use and detect reuse
when_to_use: Browser and mobile apps with long-lived sessions
trade_offs: Requires token family state
implementation_notes: Store hashed refresh tokens and revoke family on reuse

name: Session revocation list
description: Invalidate compromised or logged-out sessions before expiry
when_to_use: Enterprise SaaS admin logout and incident response
trade_offs: Adds lookup to auth flow
implementation_notes: Track token version or session id in server store

name: Step-up authentication
description: Require stronger auth for sensitive actions
when_to_use: Payments password changes exports and admin actions
trade_offs: Adds UX friction
implementation_notes: Prompt MFA before high-risk operation and record auth time

name: MFA recovery codes
description: Allow secure account recovery when MFA device is lost
when_to_use: Consumer and enterprise accounts
trade_offs: Recovery codes become sensitive credentials
implementation_notes: Hash recovery codes and show them once

name: Ownership check policy
description: Verify actor owns or can access the target resource
when_to_use: Any endpoint with path IDs or object IDs
trade_offs: Easy to miss in nested resources
implementation_notes: Centralize ownership checks in service policies

name: Deny by default authorization
description: Default every action to forbidden until explicitly allowed
when_to_use: Admin APIs multi-role apps and enterprise systems
trade_offs: Requires complete policy coverage
implementation_notes: Write tests for allowed and denied cases

name: Mass assignment protection
description: Prevent clients from setting server-controlled fields
when_to_use: User profile admin flags roles and account status
trade_offs: Requires explicit DTO mapping
implementation_notes: Map allowed input fields only and ignore or reject extras

name: Object level authorization
description: Check authorization for each object in collection and detail APIs
when_to_use: Multi-tenant APIs and shared workspaces
trade_offs: Can add query complexity
implementation_notes: Apply auth filters in repository and service policy
keywords: b object level auth bola idor

name: Output encoding
description: Encode untrusted data before rendering or returning HTML
when_to_use: APIs serving HTML emails admin panels and templates
trade_offs: Context-specific encoding needed
implementation_notes: Encode by HTML attribute URL or JS context as appropriate

name: SQL injection prevention
description: Use parameterized queries and safe query builders
when_to_use: Any SQL database access
trade_offs: Dynamic query builders still need whitelist
implementation_notes: Parameterize values and whitelist identifiers

name: SSRF guard
description: Restrict server-side outbound requests to user-provided URLs
when_to_use: Webhook testers importers image fetchers and integrations
trade_offs: Can block valid internal integrations if too strict
implementation_notes: Validate scheme resolve IP block private ranges and use egress allowlist

name: File upload scanning
description: Validate and scan uploaded files before use
when_to_use: User documents images imports and attachments
trade_offs: Scanning adds latency and cost
implementation_notes: Check MIME size extension virus scan and store outside web root

name: Tokenization of sensitive values
description: Replace sensitive identifiers with tokens
when_to_use: Payments PII and external account identifiers
trade_offs: Requires token vault or mapping service
implementation_notes: Store token in app database and keep raw value in controlled vault

name: KMS envelope encryption
description: Encrypt data keys with managed key service
when_to_use: Sensitive fields backups files and multi-tenant isolation
trade_offs: Key lifecycle needs operational care
implementation_notes: Generate data key encrypt data and store encrypted data key

name: Per-tenant encryption keys
description: Isolate tenant data cryptographically
when_to_use: Enterprise SaaS regulated tenants and BYOK
trade_offs: More complex key management and rotation
implementation_notes: Use tenant key hierarchy and key access audit

name: Audit log tamper resistance
description: Make audit logs hard to alter silently
when_to_use: Compliance finance healthcare and admin actions
trade_offs: Adds storage and verification complexity
implementation_notes: Use append-only storage hashes or external log sink

name: Security event alerting
description: Alert on suspicious auth and privilege events
when_to_use: Login abuse admin changes and data exports
trade_offs: Too noisy alerts get ignored
implementation_notes: Alert on impossible travel brute force role changes and mass export

name: Secret rotation runbook
description: Rotate secrets safely without downtime
when_to_use: API keys database passwords webhook secrets and JWT keys
trade_offs: Requires dual-secret windows
implementation_notes: Support multiple active keys and document rotation steps

name: JWT key identifier
description: Use kid header and key set for token verification
when_to_use: JWT signing key rotation and multi-service validation
trade_offs: Incorrect kid handling can create vulnerabilities
implementation_notes: Resolve kid from allowlisted key set and reject unknown keys

name: Egress allowlist
description: Restrict outbound network destinations from backend
when_to_use: High-security services and SSRF risk reduction
trade_offs: Operational overhead for new integrations
implementation_notes: Allow known hosts only and monitor denied egress

name: Mutual TLS internal services
description: Authenticate service-to-service traffic
when_to_use: Internal APIs in zero trust or regulated environments
trade_offs: Certificate lifecycle adds complexity
implementation_notes: Use mTLS with workload identity and rotation
keywords: mtls service to service zero trust

name: Data subject export workflow
description: Export user data safely for privacy requests
when_to_use: GDPR CCPA and user data portability
trade_offs: Requires data inventory
implementation_notes: Queue export job verify identity and audit delivery

name: Data deletion workflow
description: Delete or anonymize user data across systems
when_to_use: Privacy compliance account deletion and retention
trade_offs: Hard with backups and derived data
implementation_notes: Maintain deletion map outbox tasks and audit completion

name: Security regression tests
description: Keep tests for fixed vulnerabilities
when_to_use: Any app after security bug fixes
trade_offs: Requires translating findings into tests
implementation_notes: Add unit integration or E2E test before closing finding
